title: Catastrophic Injury Support
text:
Catastrophic Injury Support
Following serious injury, people often need coordinated support across healthcare, rehabilitation, family life, care, and community services.

Coordinated Support
We help keep rehabilitation, care, equipment, and professional input joined up.


Practical Planning
We focus on safety, independence, quality of life, and meaningful progress.

Discuss a Referral

title: Privacy
text:
Privacy
Case Management NI respects your privacy.
Information submitted through this website will only be used to respond to your enquiry.
Please do not submit confidential medical information unless this has been agreed in advance.

title: Areas Covered
text:
Case Management NI provides support across Northern Ireland, subject to referral needs and availability.




Belfast, Lisburn, Newtownabbey, Bangor, and surrounding areas.



Community-based support across towns and rural areas.



Enquiries from other areas are welcome and considered individually.

Make an Enquiry

title: FAQ
text:
Frequently Asked Questions

Who can make a referral?
Referrals may come from solicitors, insurers, clinicians, families, or individuals.


Do you work across Northern Ireland?
Yes. We support clients across Northern Ireland, depending on referral needs and availability.


Do you support litigation cases?
Yes. We can work alongside legal representatives, insurers, rehabilitation providers, and treating professionals.


Do you provide reports?
Reports can be provided where agreed as part of the case management process.


Can families contact you directly?
Yes. Families are welcome to make an initial enquiry.


Should I send medical information through the website?
No. Please do not send confidential medical information through the website unless this has been agreed in advance.

title: For Professionals
text:
We work collaboratively with professionals involved in rehabilitation, care planning, litigation, and community support.

Our role is to provide clear communication, practical coordination, and person-centred support.




Solicitors
Insurers
Occupational therapists
Physiotherapists
Speech and language therapists
Neuropsychologists
Consultants
GPs
Care providers
Community services






Regular liaison and practical updates where agreed.



Support plans shaped around individual needs, risks, goals, and available services.



Working alongside existing teams rather than duplicating professional input.

Discuss a Referral

title: Services
text:
We provide practical, coordinated support for people with complex needs.


A clear assessment of current needs, priorities, risks, and support requirements.



Coordinating therapy, equipment, care, and community-based support.

Working with solicitors, insurers, therapists, GPs, consultants, and care providers.

title: Family Support
text:
Families often carry much of the practical and emotional weight following serious injury or complex health changes.

We help families understand the process, coordinate support, and focus on practical next steps.




Explaining rehabilitation planning
Coordinating professionals
Supporting communication
Helping prepare for discharge or transition home
Identifying community support
Reviewing changing needs over time

title: About
text:
About Case Management NI
Case Management NI provides independent case management and rehabilitation coordination across Northern Ireland.
Our approach is professional, calm, practical, and human-centred.

We focus on what matters most to the person: independence, safety, confidence, family life, and meaningful progress.

title: Our Process
text:
Our Process
We keep the process clear, structured, and focused on practical outcomes.


1. Initial Contact
We receive the enquiry and consider whether case management support may be appropriate.


2. Discussion
We discuss the person’s current situation, priorities, risks, and support needs.


3. Assessment
Where agreed, we complete or coordinate assessment to understand needs in more detail.


4. Planning
We help develop a practical, person-centred plan.


5. Coordination
We liaise with relevant services and professionals to support progress.


Start a Referral

title: Referrals
text:
We welcome enquiries from individuals, families, solicitors, insurers, healthcare professionals, and support providers.

Our aim is to make the referral process clear, calm, and practical from the first conversation.

We liaise with relevant professionals and services to support practical progress.
